t_ch: 丹恒，你永远也无法逃离饮月，因为他是你的起点，他所犯的罪业将长伴你的前路，如影随形，直至入灭。
t_en: Dan Heng, you can never escape the shadow of Imbibitor Lunae. He is your origin, and his sins will accompany you in your path forward until you meet your eventual doom.

t_ch: 「刃」…是个好名字。在没有尽头的余生里，你只能在杀与被杀间徘徊，求索自己的埋骨之地。若非如此，你便无法消解「应星」的悔恨。
t_en: Blade... What a fitting name. In the remainder of your infinite life, you will experience only murder and your own death, all the while pleading for a place to rest. This is the only way you are able to relinquish Yingxing's regrets.

t_ch: 最后是我，我将面临联盟判令，背负永罚。而在此之后…还有更为惨重的「代价」在等待着我。
t_en: And I... will face the punishment of the Alliance and suffer an eternal punishment... before paying an even worse price...

t_ch: 唯有如此，那些当被铭记的痛苦…才不会逝去。
t_en: That is the only way to keep the memory of the pain of the past... from fading away.

t_ch: 「云上五骁」…该是彼此告别的时候了。
t_en: High-Cloud Quintet... It is time to say goodbye.

t_ch: 等等！镜流，在你离开之前，你还欠我一份报酬。
t_en: Wait! Jingliu, before you leave, you still owe me my due.

t_ch: 我试过，除了在你身上多留些伤口，我帮不了你更多。
t_en: I have tried. Besides inflicting more wounds on your body, there is nothing else I can do for you.

t_ch: 你的不死身绝不是这么简单就能被打发的东西。以人间的剑杀不死神使的血肉，这一点，「命运的奴隶」应该告诉过你吧？
t_en: The immortality that you possess is not something that can be dealt with so easily. I am sure Destiny's Slave already told you that the swords of mortals are incapable of killing someone with the flesh of an Emanator.

t_ch: 他说过。但你依然欠我这一剑。
t_en: He did... but you still owe me the attempt.

t_ch: 我教你剑时就已说过啦。我不对全无生趣、引颈待戮的人动手——
t_en: I told you when I taught you the way of the sword — I am uninterested in drawing my blade against someone who does not long for life... who wants only to be slaughtered—

t_ch: ——只有对手才能让你拔剑。
t_en: You will only draw against an opponent.

t_ch: 镜流，我来奉还你的一剑之教。
t_en: Jingliu, allow me to repay you for your teachings.

t_ch: 珍惜此刻吧，我给你短暂一死的机会。
t_en: Savor the moment. I will deliver you a brief moment of death.

t_ch: 为什么…为什么只有孽物能一遍遍卷土重来……
t_en: Why... why is it that only the abominations can return time and time again...?

t_ch: 为什么她这样的人却要被埋葬，被烧成灰烬，被人遗忘…为什么？！
t_en: Why does someone like her have to be buried, burned to ash, and eventually forgotten...? Why!?

t_ch: 最后，你刺出穿心一击，把我和剑一起留在了那片枯冢。
t_en: In the end, you unleashed the final piercing blow, and left both me and the sword in the withered grave.

t_ch: 奇妙的是，在这短暂如梦的死亡里，我不再感到那么痛苦了……
t_en: The miraculous thing is that in my brief moment of death... all the pain went away...

t_ch: …就像那些举杯痛饮的日子一样。
t_en: ...like when we drank to drown our sorrows...

t_ch: 与此同时，神策府中…
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 汪汪，汪汪汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof, howoof woof woof woof!

t_ch: 请讲人话。
t_en: Please speak in human.

t_ch: 汪汪，汪汪汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof, woof woof woof woof!

t_ch: 谛听没有理会你的无理请求。
t_en: Diting ignores your unreasonable request.

t_ch: 汪汪汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof woof!

t_ch: 汪汪汪？汪～
t_en: Woof woof woof? Woof~

t_ch: 很显然你蒙对了一些词汇，与谛听产生了交流。不过，这显然还不够。
t_en: Evidently, you guessed some vocabulary right and communicated with Diting. However, this is not enough.

t_ch: 喵喵喵喵！
t_en: Meow meow meow!

t_ch: 汪呜？汪汪，汪汪汪汪！
t_en: Ruff? Woof woof, ruff ruff woof!

t_ch: 很显然你搞错了应该「喵喵喵喵」的对象。
t_en: Clearly, you are meowing up the wrong tree.

t_ch: 小吃摊的话，在那边儿。
t_en: The food stall is over there.

t_ch: 汪汪，汪汪汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof, ruff ruff woof!

t_ch: 也许谛听根本就没想去找小吃摊。
t_en: Maybe Diting was not looking for a food stall.

t_ch: 呜汪，汪汪汪呜汪汪嗷！
t_en: Woof woof, howoof woof ruff woof!

t_ch: （找一个能听懂的人吧）
t_en: (Find someone who can understand)

t_ch: 说到能够听懂谛听汪汪叫的人，你立马想起了那个人……
t_en: Speaking of someone who can understand Diting's woofs, you immediately think of...

t_ch: 三月七！
t_en: March 7th!

t_ch: 没错，就是这位姑娘。
t_en: That's right, she's the one.

t_ch: 粉色头发的美少女…
t_en: A certain cute pink-haired girl...

t_ch: 「美少女和可爱小狗之间的心灵感应」…啊，那不就是三月七说的吗？
t_en: "The psychic bond between cute girls and cute puppies" ...Hey, isn't that what March 7th said?

t_ch: 谁都没有想起…
t_en: Nothing to think of...

t_ch: 你再仔细想想，当时停云将谛听交给你们，小谛听「呜汪」了一声……
t_en: Try and remember, when Tingyun handed Diting to you, and Diting woofed...

t_ch: 三月七却说「它说港口风急，要用气味追踪可不容易。」
t_en: March 7th said, "He says the wind is too strong here at the port, so it's not easy to follow the scent."

t_ch: 三月七是能和谛听交流的。
t_en: March 7th can communicate with Diting.

t_ch: （要不，就这样不管了吧）
t_en: (What if, I just leave it be)

t_ch: 呜汪，汪～
t_en: Woof, woooof~

t_ch: 你是一个有责任心的人，不能让它留在这里不管…干脆带它去找三月七吧。
t_en: Being the responsible person you are, you can't just leave it here... How about asking March 7th for help?

t_ch: 嗷呜？
t_en: Awooo?

t_ch: 走吧，我带你去找三月七。
t_en: Let's go, I'll bring you to March 7th.

t_ch: 三月七
t_en: March 7th

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么啦？
t_en: Everything okay, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 三月，来帮我翻译翻译…
t_en: March, can you help me translate something?

t_ch: 可恶，为什么是{NICKNAME}收到了小谛听的呼叫？难道不应该是咱这样的美少女更容易得到小猫小狗的信任吗？
t_en: Hmph! Why is Diting talking to you? I've always been nice to cute little animals!

t_ch: 这突如其来的攀比心…
t_en: This isn't a contest...

t_ch: 你不懂！可爱的事物之间总是会相互吸引的。
t_en: You wouldn't understand! All cute things in the world should share a mutual affinity!

t_ch: 所以你懂它的话，对吧？
t_en: So you understand what it's saying, right?

t_ch: 当然啦，可爱的事物之间总是能心灵相通的。
t_en: Of course I understand! All cute things in the world share a special connection, after all.

t_ch: 在追捕卡芙卡的时候，第一次见到这小家伙，我就觉得咱们之间一定有某种奇妙的缘分。它随便叫唤几声，我就能听出它有几个意思。
t_en: The first time we met Diting was during the Kafka hunt. I could tell there was a mysterious connection between us — whenever he barked, I understood what he wanted to say.

t_ch: …就比如这一句。「呜汪，汪汪汪呜汪汪嗷！」
t_en: ...For example, "Woof woof, woof woof woof woof!"

t_ch: 意思就是：「尊敬的{NICKNAME}，您好，我在流云渡港口的某个地方发现了一个奇怪的东西。您也许有兴趣瞧瞧？」
t_en: Meaning: "Hello, dear {NICKNAME}. I found a strange thing at the port of Cloudford. You may want to come and have a look for yourself."

t_ch: 你是怎么翻出这么长一串的？
t_en: You got all that from a few woofs?

t_ch: 别看小谛听只是叫了几声，它发言的信息量可是非常大的哟。
t_en: Diting can convey a lot of information with only a few sounds.

t_ch: 你该不会…有谛听血脉吧？
t_en: You wouldn't happen to be... related to Diting?

t_ch: 喂，这算是拐着弯人身攻击了吧！
t_en: Hey, enough with the passive aggression!

t_ch: 总之，我就是能懂谛听的意思。
t_en: The simple truth of the matter is that I can understand him.

t_ch: 所以，小谛听到底在港口发现了什么啊？{NICKNAME}，咱们一起去瞧瞧吗？
t_en: So, what did Diting find at the port? Come on {NICKNAME}, let's go check it out.

t_ch: 原来…在你眼中我不算美少女么？
t_en: ...Aaaand why not me?

t_ch: {F#算是算啦…但，能和小动物沟通可是稀有设定！}{M#呃…你是这么看自己的吗？好吧，那勉强算啦。如果你强烈要求，我也可以把「勉强」去掉。}
t_en: {F#I think I'm a better fit, that's all. Ugh, never mind, no need to get offended.}{M#I think I'm a better fit, that's all. Ugh, never mind, no need to get offended.}

t_ch: 三月七
t_en: March 7th

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 呜呜汪，汪汪！
t_en: Woof! Woof woof!

t_ch: 小谛听说，它们一直奉命追着仙舟上各种线索到处跑，一旦发现了奇怪的东西，就会立刻上报。
t_en: Little Diting says he's been dutifully searching for clues all over the Xianzhou. He notified us as soon as he found something strange.

t_ch: 所以，它想带我们看什么东西？
t_en: So what does he want us to take a look at?

t_ch: 调查
t_en: Investigate

t_ch: 一枚玉扣静静躺在地上，浅绿的纹理闪过一抹亮彩，在空气中勾勒出一名少年的身形。
t_en: A jade tracer lying on the ground. Its light green vein flashes out an image of a teenager in the air.

t_ch: 这里是彦卿，景元将军的侍卫。
t_en: This is Yanqing, retainer to General Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 事起仓促，我正在亲自追捕一名潜逃的要犯，为了防止失联，我会留下这枚「示迹玉扣」记录行踪。
t_en: I'm in active pursuit of a fugitive. The situation is unpredictable and developing rapidly, so I've planted this jade tracer to record my whereabouts in case I lose contact.

t_ch: 若有人发现，请将它送往神策府，以便云骑军及时策应。
t_en: If anyone finds this tracer, please take it to the Seat of Divine Foresight so the Cloud Knights have the necessary intel to act swiftly.

t_ch: 是个记录装置。小谛听一定是接到了它发出的信号，才会拉咱们到这儿来瞧一瞧。
t_en: So it's a recording device. Diting must have heard its signal and called us over here to investigate.

t_ch: 连将军的侍卫都出动了…
t_en: Even the general's retainer is getting in on the action...

t_ch: 看他的模样，也就八九岁。这么点儿大的人已经当上了将军的侍卫，仙舟上的孩子还真了不得。
t_en: He looks about eight or nine years old! And he's already the general's retainer? The Xianzhou really does have some child prodigies, huh.

t_ch: 让一个孩子去缉拿要犯…？
t_en: They sent a child to apprehend a major criminal...?

t_ch: 人不可貌相，小小年纪就能当上将军的侍卫，一定有什么过人之处。
t_en: Can't disqualify him just for being a widdle baby! If he's the general's retainer, there must be more to him than meets the eye.

t_ch: 像咱们这样，也不是小小年纪就登上了列车，踏上开拓之旅……
t_en: We're not that old ourselves, right? And yet here we are — Express passengers on trailblazing expeditions!

t_ch: 也是，仙舟上狗都能派活儿…
t_en: Yep, even the dogs here can give out missions. Nothing shocks me now.

t_ch: 咱俩也不能算是小小年纪了…
t_en: I don't think the two of us count as "widdle babies..."

t_ch: 这节骨眼上能让将军侍卫穷追不舍的「要犯」，恐怕也只有星核猎手了。不知这个少年的追捕行动顺不顺利？
t_en: Given everything that's been happening on the Xianzhou recently, I reckon the only "fugitive" worthy of a retainer's attention... would be a Stellaron Hunter. I wonder how he's getting along?

t_ch: 翻阅行动记录
t_en: Check operations log

t_ch: 离开
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 这就…完了？行动记录里也没有下文了？
t_en: And that's... the end? There's nothing else in the operations log?

t_ch: 记录作者外出取材了…
t_en: Writer's block perhaps...

t_ch: 这也不是连载故事啊…
t_en: Who doesn't like a good cliffhanger?

t_ch: 出现在他记录里叫「镜流」的女人，看起来好危险。罗浮仙舟这次星核危机，到底是引来了多少怪人。
t_en: That Jingliu seems pretty dangerous... *sigh* The Stellaron crisis sure has attracted a lot of strange people to the Luofu.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们是不是得赶快把这个东西交到景元的手里？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, shouldn't we hand this over to Jing Yuan as soon as possible?

t_ch: 呜汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 呜汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 我们找到了彦卿留下的东西...
t_en: We found something left behind by Yanqing...

t_ch: 景元的留影挥手拂过，玉扣爆发出一阵亮光，他的虚幻身形也随之闪动。
t_en: Jing Yuan's hologram waves. The jade tracer emits a beam of light as the illusory figure flickers.

t_ch: 这的确是彦卿的示迹玉扣，不胜感谢。
t_en: Without a doubt — this jade tracer belongs to Yanqing. I can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 镜流是谁？
t_en: Who is Jingliu?

t_ch: 你们…已读过其中的记录了吗？
t_en: You... already accessed the recordings?

t_ch: 呃…{NICKNAME}只是不小心点进去——
t_en: Uh...{NICKNAME} accidentally pressed a button...

t_ch: 无妨。她是我的授艺恩师，我在云骑军时，她是我的上司。不过，她已离开仙舟许久了……
t_en: ...No harm done. She was my mentor and my superior when I was a member of the Cloud Knights. However, she left the Xianzhou a long time ago...

t_ch: （景元沉默良久）
t_en: (Jing Yuan is silent for a while)

t_ch: 若是见到她，你们最好别靠近，立刻告诉我。
t_en: If you see her, be sure to keep your distance and notify me immediately.

t_ch: 彦卿还安全吗？
t_en: Is Yanqing safe?

t_ch: 他接下追拿要犯的任务，生死由不得我来操心。云骑上阵，从来如此。
t_en: He took on the charge of pursuing a major criminal — it is not my place to impede him for fear of his wellbeing. That is the nature of the Cloud Knights and their duty.

t_ch: 不过，我对他有信心。
t_en: I do have confidence in him, though.

t_ch: 关于刃的事情…
t_en: About Blade...

t_ch: 恕我不能透露，见谅。
t_en: *sigh* Forgive me, but I cannot disclose this information.

t_ch: 没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 多谢你们的帮助，列车团的朋友。时局变化无常，各位多加小心。
t_en: Thank you again for your help, friends from the Express. The situation remains unpredictable, so take care.

t_ch: 希望能在一切问题安然解决后，再与诸位欢饮畅谈。
t_en: I hope to be able to drink and talk freely with you once again when everything is settled.

t_ch: 景元的影子看向你。你不确定这究竟是某种自动答录影像，还是他本人随时回话的远程投影。
t_en: Jing Yuan's apparition turned to you. You are not sure whether this is some kind of automatic audio-visual answering technique, or a real-time remote materialization from himself.

t_ch: 打扰啦，队长。
t_en: Captain, could you spare a moment?

t_ch: 小弟弟，怎么此刻不在神策府呆着，倒是关心起咱们一线的行动来了？
t_en: Yanqing? Why are you all the way out here on the front lines? Shouldn't you be back at the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 将军操心犯难的事儿太多了，身为侍卫，来这儿当然是要为将军分忧的……
t_en: The general already has a lot on his plate. As his retainer, it's my duty to share the burden...

t_ch: 目前一无所获，对吧？
t_en: Nothing new to report, I assume?

t_ch: 哼哼，那个「刃」从幽囚狱脱出禁制后，就像蒸发了一般。或许他此刻已不在罗浮仙舟了？
t_en: Correct. After this "Blade" escaped from The Shackling Prison, he disappeared into thin air. I wonder if he's even still on the ship?

t_ch: 又或许，刃根本没打算逃走。
t_en: Or perhaps he had no intention of escaping in the first place.

t_ch: 唉，罗浮这是招惹了什么煞星，妖魔鬼怪一齐作乱。
t_en: *sigh* What foul demons found their way across the stars to wreak havoc on the Luofu?

t_ch: 我就是来为将军除妖的。
t_en: That's why I'm here — to help the general eliminate those demons.

t_ch: 你？可我没接到景元将军的饬令……
t_en: Is that so? I didn't receive any orders from the general.

t_ch: 这…是秘密行动。
t_en: Ahem... This is a covert operation.

t_ch: 那要不要加派几个人给侍卫大人当援手？
t_en: ...Then shall we dispatch a few knights for this "operation" of yours?

t_ch: 队长的好意我就心领了。秘密的意思，就是知情人越少越好。
t_en: I appreciate the gesture... but the fewer people who know about a covert operation, the better.

t_ch: 大张旗鼓只会打草惊蛇。好猎人向来独来独往。
t_en: Making a big show of it will only scare the snake deeper into the grass... A good hunter must operate alone.

t_ch: 关于刃的追查记录，线索中断在星槎海。
t_en: Blade tracking entry: Traces run cold at Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 得向那儿的云骑打听打听……
t_en: I'll have to ask the Cloud Knights in the area if they saw anything...

t_ch: 对了，犯人要在各个洞天间穿行，还得依靠星槎。
t_en: Hmm... A fugitive would still have to rely on starskiffs to travel between different delves.

t_ch: 从被他劫走的星槎找起，也许能有眉目……
t_en: If we start by investigating the starskiff he stole, maybe we can get a lead...

t_ch: 猎物既然留下痕迹，那就好办了。
t_en: The best prey is one that leaves traces.

t_ch: 来吧，谛听，顺着气息找一找。
t_en: Here Diting, let's follow the scent!

t_ch: 怎么还有被困着的百姓？这里的云骑办事不力啊。
t_en: Civilians are still getting attacked? Where are the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 喂，你别慌，我这就救你出来！
t_en: Hey, don't panic, I'll get you out of there!

t_ch: 怪了，这几个是什么时候倒的？
t_en: Huh? They were on their feet a second ago...

t_ch: 多谢你出手相救，小弟弟。
t_en: Thank you for your assistance, young man.

t_ch: 喔，那是我份内之事。罗浮的港口封锁了，你怎么还一个人在这儿？
t_en: Just doing my job. The port has been sealed off, why are you here alone?

t_ch: 我随一艘商船来到这儿。最近过去几个老朋友的影子，一个个在我脑袋里打转。我想和老朋友们碰上一面，重温旧时光……
t_en: I came with a merchant ship. The shadows of old friends have returned to my thoughts of late... I came to reunite with them and relive old times...

t_ch: 谁料到罗浮现在这么凶险了呢？
t_en: Who would have imagined the Luofu could become such a dangerous place?

t_ch: 那你来得不巧，仙舟出了点意外。不过要不了多久，将军就会解决的。走吧，你不能待在这儿，咱们得去最近的云骑驻所。
t_en: You chose a bad time for a visit I'm afraid — there's been an incident. It won't take long for the general to fix it, though. This place isn't safe — we should head to the nearest Cloud Knight garrison.

t_ch: 对了，你有没有见过一个黑衣长发的男人——
t_en: By the way, did you see a man with long hair and black clothes around here...?

t_ch: 你…你看不见吗？抱歉，我还以为……
t_en: You... you can't see? Apologies, I thought...

t_ch: 我叫彦卿，是正式录名在籍的云骑军。还没请教大姐姐的名字？
t_en: My name is Yanqing — I'm registered with the Cloud Knights. And your name is...?

t_ch: 我叫镜流。
t_en: Jingliu.

t_ch: 呃…镜流姐姐，我先领你走一段吧。可能要绕点路，但我保证把你平安送到云骑那里。
t_en: Nice to meet you. Um... Miss Jingliu, allow me to lead the way. We might have to take a bit of a detour, but I promise to get you to the Cloud Knights safely.

t_ch: 接下来怎么走？
t_en: Where do we go from here?

t_ch: 嘘，安静。有麻烦要对付。
t_en: Shh, quiet... We've got trouble to deal with first.

t_ch: 大姐姐，你待在此处不要走动，我去去就回。
t_en: Wait here a moment, miss — I'll be right back.

t_ch: 这些家伙是什么来头？唉，将军说仙舟另有内患，果然不错。
t_en: Where did these guys come from? Huh, the general was right — there's a threat lurking on the Xianzhou itself.

t_ch: 小弟弟，你剑术不错哦。
t_en: You are quite the swordmaster, young man.

t_ch: 你…看得见？
t_en: You... you saw that?

t_ch: 我能听到。飞剑破空的鸣动，锐锋切割的声响…这些痕迹都会在无形中流露出剑艺的优劣。
t_en: I heard it. A sword whistles through the air and rings out upon contacting the enemy ... Whilst invisible to the eye, such signals reveal the quality of one's swordplay.

t_ch: 就像乐师听琴，诗人听韵。剑招变化流转之间，高明的剑士绝不会留下滞涩的杂音。能在一息间同时控御六柄飞剑，有这般实力的云骑应该屈指可数了。
t_en: As a musician listens to the notes of a song, so a poet listens to its rhyme... and in the flow of combat, a skilled swordmaster delivers both with their blade. I'll wager the Cloud Knights capable of wielding six flying swords at a time number few indeed.

t_ch: 呃，哈哈，过奖过奖。
t_en: Uh, haha, thanks miss.

t_ch: 不过一意强攻，不知藏锋……
t_en: However, your zealousness inhibits your ability to hide your line of attack....

t_ch: 唔？
t_en: Huh...?

t_ch: 因此你的剑曲，收尾处多少显得杂乱了。
t_en: ...which causes your sword's song to become somewhat mumbled at the end.

t_ch: …看来琴曲与剑术当真有相通之处呢。将军也评过类似的话，说我的剑洋洋意气，棱角过盛，想要夺得「剑首」之名，还欠一分成熟…
t_en: ...I guess the connection between music and swordplay is real after all. The general told me something similar — he said my swordplay was too proud and angular. He said it lacked the maturity required to win the title of Sword Champion...

t_ch: 剑首？我记得，那是云骑军中剑术登峰造极之人的头衔。太遥远了……
t_en: Sword Champion? If I recall correctly, that title is bestowed on one who reaches the pinnacle of swordsmanship in the Cloud Knights. But that was a long time ago...

t_ch: 是呀，打从「饮月之乱」后，罗浮的剑首就一直空悬着，不过，待到罗浮云骑部队巡猎归来，演武仪典再开，这头衔我是志在必得。
t_en: Right! Ever since the Sedition of Imbibitor Lunae, the title of Sword Champion has gone unclaimed. But once everything settles down and the Combat Arts Ceremony returns, it's a title I'm determined to win!

t_ch: 云骑军中的武艺各有传承。小弟弟，你的剑术又是谁指点的？
t_en: The Cloud Knights have many martial arts traditions. Who was it that instructed you in swordplay, young man?

t_ch: 姐姐既是赏剑之人，我就不卖关子了。正是罗浮的景元将军。
t_en: I see you're no stranger to the art — I won't keep you in suspense. My master is none other than General Jing Yuan of the Luofu.

t_ch: 将军……
t_en: General...

t_ch: 就算你很久没来罗浮，也该在外听说过景元将军的威名吧？虽然将军总说自己不擅用剑，技艺生疏…但每次教起我来，他总是起劲的很。
t_en: I know you haven't visited for a long time, but surely you've heard of General Jing Yuan? He says he has no affinity with the sword — that his skills are getting rusty... Well, if that's true, I never noticed from his training.

t_ch: （哼笑）
t_en: Hm.

t_ch: 是了，星槎的航行记录都登录在码头的舵航仪里。
t_en: The starskiff voyage log can be accessed through the dock navigator array.

t_ch: 还在通航的星槎…有了！
t_en: Active starskiff, active starskiff... there!

t_ch: 这一艘行经太卜司与工造司……
t_en: This one goes past the Divination Commission and the Artisanship Commission...

t_ch: 好了，我的事办完了。该送姐姐去安全的地方了。
t_en: Well, my work here is finished. Now to make sure you get to a safe destination.

t_ch: 现今时候，云骑驻地也算不上安全吧。
t_en: Given the present circumstances, the Cloud Knight garrison probably isn't a safe destination, wouldn't you say?

t_ch: 对，所以不去云骑驻地，而是直接送你去幽囚狱，包吃包住，还有重兵护卫着，绝对安全。
t_en: Correct, so we're not going there. I'm taking you to The Shackling Prison. Safe, well-guarded, plenty of food, and a place to sleep.

t_ch: …小弟弟，要拿人总该有个说法吧。
t_en: ...Young man, if you wish to apprehend someone, shouldn't you have a reason?

t_ch: 行迹可疑，藏头露尾，只这一条就够了。你该不会觉得我是小孩子，就很好糊弄吧？
t_en: ...Suspicious behavior and half-truths — those alone are reason enough. You think you can fool me just because I'm a child?

t_ch: 且不谈封锁的港口怎么突然多出一个被困的旅客。这一路走来，我瞧你步子轻捷稳健，哪儿有半点盲人的样子。
t_en: A sealed-off port with a stranded passenger? Unlikely. Not to mention you walking the whole way here without so much as a stumble — you can see as well as I can.

t_ch: 至于剑法，你用耳朵听个头头是道也就罢了，连我御剑的数目也能报的一柄不差。这份见识，哪是普通人能有的？
t_en: The biggest giveaway was you comment about my swordplay. Correctly guessing the number of swords from the sound alone? You'd have to be more than human.

t_ch: 你根本不是盲人，对不对？
t_en: You're not blind at all, are you?

t_ch: 我从没说过眼睛看不见。是你见我黑纱遮眼，想当然罢了。
t_en: I never claimed to be. You came up with that yourself.
Uh...

t_ch: 不要紧的，小弟弟，我和你无冤无仇，也不曾想对仙舟不利啊。这罩黑纱，正是我不愿触景生情，身陷魔阴，再造狂孽的证明。
t_en: Fear not, young man, I hold no grievance against you and have never held ill intent towards the Xianzhou. The black veil covering my eyes is merely proof of my resolve to never look back, to never fall again into mara and destruction.

t_ch: 我来这里，只为捉一个人，和你同行倒是正好。
t_en: I came here to catch one person. It's quite fortunate that our paths have crossed.

t_ch: 你也是…为了「刃」来的？
t_en: The one you're looking for... is it Blade?

t_ch: 「刃」，这是他现在的名字吗？弃身锋刃，刀剑研心，倒是会挑名字呵。
t_en: He goes by "Blade" nowadays? A fitting name for someone who dedicated his mind and body to the ways of the sword.

t_ch: 带我去见他，小弟弟。
t_en: Take me to him, young man.

t_ch: 你不是我的对手，所以，你也不是「刃」的对手。有我随你同行，才不会枉送性命，小弟弟。
t_en: You are no match for me, and therefore, no match for Blade either. Let me accompany you — there is no need to throw your life away.

t_ch: 剑芒未出，怎知胜负高下？劝你别小瞧我的剑。
t_en: Hmph, we've not even drawn swords yet and you declare yourself the winner? Let me offer you a word of advice — don't underestimate me.

t_ch: 我不想和云骑军同室操戈，不如这样——
t_en: *sigh* I was hoping to avoid a conflict with the Cloud Knights. How about this...

t_ch: 咱们来比一场，就用如今遍布罗浮仙舟的孽物试剑。瞧瞧谁的剑更快，斩的更多，如何？
t_en: Let's have ourselves a little contest. We can use the abominations that have infested the Luofu as target practice... and see whose sword can slay more, and slay faster.

t_ch: 要是我赢了……？
t_en: And if I win...?

t_ch: 我当然愿赌服输，乖乖就缚，去幽囚狱受审，任由处置。
t_en: Then I will obediently follow you to The Shackling Prison and receive whatever judgment awaits me there. Don't worry, I'll keep my end of the bargain.

t_ch: 但要是我胜了，你就要同我分享刃的行踪。如何？
t_en: But if I win, you have to share Blade's whereabouts with me. Fair enough?

t_ch: 云骑不拿公务做交易，何况，你赢不了。
t_en: A Cloud Knight never gambles with official business. But in any case, you won't beat me.

t_ch: 我喜欢你的自信。不过「剑芒未出，怎知胜负高下」，对不对？
t_en: I admire your confidence. But what was it you said earlier about declaring a winner before swords were drawn?

t_ch: 这儿的孽物怕是已被你剿灭干净了，咱们不妨换个地方。
t_en: It would seem we've cleared every abomination from the area. Let's find another location and continue our contest.

t_ch: 呜汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 「加派几个人」…嘿，瞧不起谁呢。
t_en: "Dispatch a few knights..." Hmph, how incapable does he think I am?

t_ch: 现在看我年纪小，等再过一阵子……
t_en: I might be young now, but just you wait...

t_ch: 看来星核邪祟的影响在逐渐加深……
t_en: Looks like the influence of the Stellaron spirits is growing...

t_ch: 天舶司乱成一团，丢了的船也不知能不能找回。
t_en: Tsk, the Sky-Faring Commission really made a mess of this — who knows if we'll ever find all these missing ships.

t_ch: 太卜整天吹嘘自己未卜先知。这回的祸事算出来了吗？
t_en: The master diviner is always boasting about her foresight. Didn't manage to foresee this disaster, did she?

t_ch: 小弟弟，咱们这是往哪儿走？
t_en: Where exactly are we headed, young man?

t_ch: 去码头。放心，你不会有危险的。
t_en: To the docks. Don't worry, you'll be safe.

t_ch: 大姐姐是从其他仙舟来的么？是「曜青」，还是「方壶」？
t_en: So are you from one of the other Xianzhou ships? The Yaoqing? The Fanghu?

t_ch: 都不是，我来自苍城。
t_en: ...I'm from the Cangcheng.

t_ch: 「苍城」？我怎么没听过，六座仙舟里有叫这个名字的吗？
t_en: The Cangcheng? There's a ship called the Cangcheng? Why haven't I heard of it...?

t_ch: 大姐姐，你多久没回罗浮仙舟了啊？
t_en: Miss Jingliu, how long has it been since you last set foot on the Luofu?

t_ch: 你平时也这么健谈吗？
t_en: Are you always this talkative?

t_ch: 啊…只是觉得出个声响，方便你知道我在哪里。
t_en: Uh... Just making conversation — I wanted you to know I was still here.

t_ch: 好了，附近安全了。咱们接着走。
t_en: Alright, the coast is clear. Let's keep moving.

t_ch: 有什么本事，尽管让我瞧瞧。
t_en: Show me what you've got.

t_ch: 我…我接下了她的剑？
t_en: I... I deflected it?

t_ch: 你找到的那些记录我就拿走了。
t_en: I'll take those records you found.

t_ch: 若是机巧鸟还能正常运作，也许可以找到一些影像记录…现在怕是不成了。
t_en: If the cycrane hadn't been damaged, we could've extracted some footage... *sigh* That's no longer an option.

t_ch: 如此严密的包围网，却没能找到犯人的踪迹，恐怕他暂时也不想离开罗浮。
t_en: The perimeter is well guarded, but there are no traces of the fugitive passing through. It seems he has no intention of leaving the Luofu for the time being.

t_ch: 看来得往码头走一遭了。
t_en: Guess it's back to the docks again.

t_ch: 从哪儿来…
t_en: (Where did she come from...?)

t_ch: 离开多久了…
t_en: (How long has she been away...?)

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: (Let's get going...)

t_ch: 留神脚下的路，大姐姐。咱们走吧。
t_en: Let's get going, miss. Careful — take your time.

t_ch: 谢啦，小弟弟。
t_en: Thank you, young man.

t_ch: 彦卿走了几步又退了回来，还是应该回去叫上蒙眼女子，让她跟随同行为好。
t_en: Yanqing marched several steps forward then returned — It'd better take the lady with blindfold together on the road.

t_ch: 星槎不能进出。重犯也走不脱。
t_en: Starskiffs are blocked from entering or exiting. There's no way the fugitive got through...

t_ch: 但他若向仙舟内逃窜…这么大的地界，很难抓得着他。
t_en: But if he fled towards the interior of the ship... it'll be hard to track him down over such a vast area.

t_ch: 对了，犯人要在各个洞天间穿行，还得依靠星槎。
t_en: Hmm... A fugitive would still have to rely on starskiffs to travel between different delves.

t_ch: 从被他劫走的星槎找起，也许能有眉目……
t_en: If we start by investigating the starskiff he stole, maybe we can get a lead...

t_ch: 看来得往码头走一遭了。
t_en: Guess it's back to the docks again.

t_ch: 这伤痕，不是云骑军留下的。
t_en: This kind of wound... is not the work of a Cloud Knight.

t_ch: 猎物既然留下痕迹，那就好办了。
t_en: The best prey is one that leaves traces.

t_ch: 来吧，谛听，顺着气息找一找。
t_en: Here Diting, let's follow the scent!

t_ch: 这是……？
t_en: What's this...?

t_ch: 是了，星槎的航行记录都登录在码头的舵航仪里。
t_en: The starskiff voyage log can be accessed through the dock navigator array.

t_ch: 伪装假死么，糊弄小孩的把戏……
t_en: Playing dead? What a childish trick...

t_ch: 戳一戳…
t_en: Poke...

t_ch: 等等看…
t_en: Wait a second...

t_ch: 僵卧不动的遗骸微微颤抖起来，空洞的眼神中突然现出一股怒意。
t_en: The stiffed remains started trembling with a sudden anger out from its hollow eyes.

t_ch: 唔，文笔奇烂，节奏还拖沓…不对，我在干什么啊？
t_en: Eh, bad writing, terrible pacing... Wait, what was I supposed to be doing again?

t_ch: 彦卿啊彦卿，一本小说就拖住了你。竟把将军的嘱托抛在脑后……
t_en: Focus, Yanqing! You can't let novels distract from your duty to the general... especially not ones as crummy as this...

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 长船中心的静室里，狼首人身的尊者自玄思中睁开双眼，预感到宿命之敌的来临。
t_en: In a silent room in the middle of the fleet, a wolf-head Master broke from his meditation and opened his eyes, facing up his fatal enemy.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 此刻，长身玉立的少女就站在甬道尽头，如同一把脱鞘之剑，锋锐逼人。
t_en: At the moment, a graceful young lady was standing at the end of the corridor, like a sharp sword unleashing from its sheath.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 她的周遭，无数光点明灭闪烁，呼吸着、律动着，仿佛置身于银河。定睛细看，那些光源被封存在一个个半透明的舱罐中，好似数不清的神龛供奉在四壁上。
t_en: Countless spots of light encircled her, like stars breathing and dancing in the cosmos. If you look closer, you may find that the light spots are sealed in a translucent jar, like shrines immured on walls.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「这些丹腑…」狼首的智者吐气开声。「便是你们自神迹中得到的好处。」
t_en: "These Core Esse..." The wolf-head Master uttered, "So they are what you obtained from the divine relic."

t_ch: （下一页）
t_en: (Next page)

t_ch: （放下小说）
t_en: (Put the novel down)

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「长生主只给了我族深不可测的力量，但却在你们体内播撒神木的种子，当真不公平啊……」
t_en: "The Master of Immortality only granted us power, but cultivated the seed of the sacred tree in you. How unfair..."

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「我斩阵三万人，命解骨师自他们的遗体中剖走了尚有活性的丹腑，筑成了这片腑海神龛，详加钻研。」
t_en: "I put thirty thousand people to death and had the anatomist dissect their live Core Esses to build all these Esse Shrines, so that I can make further studies of them."

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「我想的没错。若说心脏的存在是为了维持生命，那丹腑的意义便在于超越生命，自根源汲纳力量。它是比心脏更重要的器官，是潜藏于尔等身体里的『引擎』。」
t_en: "My studies have proven me right. The heart is for the sake of sustaining life, and Core Esse is to absorb power from the root. This organ is much more important than the heart. It can be regarded as the 'engine' of the body."

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「我用它们来驱动你脚下这艘千足之舟，用你们的身体作为武器来毁灭联盟！」
t_en: "Now I am using them to propel this fleet, using their organs to destroy their own alliance!"

t_ch: （下一页）
t_en: (Next page)

t_ch: （放下小说）
t_en: (Put the novel down)

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 狼首人掀开遮护胸前的华美罩袍，一袭形如水母般半透明的胞衣铠甲，紧紧包覆着贲突虬结的肌肉。
t_en: The wolf-head man lifted off the gorgeous mantle that covered his chest, and revealed a jellyfish-like translucent armor coating his bulging muscles.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 他深深吐纳，仿佛能一口饮尽这片狭小空间中的空气。数息之间，佝偻的身形拔节撑开，成为铁塔般昂藏的巨躯。
t_en: He inhaled deeply, as if he could consume all the air within this narrow space. In seconds, his hunched body expanded muscle by muscle, and turned into a huge iron tower of flesh.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 因易形而碎裂的骨肉发出令人牙根酸疼的摩擦声，又迅速弥合。这些痛楚，狼首人早习以为常。
t_en: The cracking bones and warping flesh generated a sharp and painful noise during the deformation, and quickly healed. The wolf-head man has long been used to these pains.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 随着呼吸，细弱的光丝自他的口鼻眼耳中流溢而出。在他高大的躯体上，熟悉的丹腑光芒开始大放异彩。
t_en: As he breathed, a weak glimmer of light overflowed from his mouth, nose, eyes, and ears. His Core Esse started to shine brightly.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「这身金丹玄甲，与我的血肉适性匹配无缺，简直就像是自个身上长出来的器官一般。但它究竟能发挥出几成功力，始终是个谜。」
t_en: "This armor fits well my blood and flesh. It is like it were an organ attached to my body. However, its prowess is yet to be tested."

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 「那些与我对阵的家伙，不过一击就成了肉泥。但换做云上五骁的英雄，也许能试出这个问题的答案吧！」
t_en: "My opponents have all turned into balls of flesh with just one blow. Maybe the High-Cloud Quintet can help me know its true limit!"

t_ch: （下一页）
t_en: (Next page)

t_ch: （放下小说）
t_en: (Put the novel down)

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 女子的眼神如坚冰般灰暗冷酷，仿佛眼前发生的一切无聊之极，不值一提。
t_en: The lady's eyes were as dark and cold as ice, as if the things happening in front of her were dull and uninteresting.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 三万个将士，三万颗丹腑，三万点生命的烛火……她竭力不让自己被眼前残酷的奇观夺走心神。
t_en: Thirty thousand soldiers, thirty thousand Core Esse that are kindles of life... She tried not to get distracted by the cruelty of the miracle before her eyes.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 敌我对峙的感应有如天平之微妙，一侧微倾，另一侧必挟势而上。抓住这稍纵即逝的机会，狼首人模糊成一团灰色的暴风，裂地而来。
t_en: The subtle balance between the two sides is hard to maintain. One would take their advantage precisely when they get the chance. The wolf-head man soared forward as a mass of gray storm.

s_en: A Novel Left Behind By Tourists
t_ch: 下一个心跳间，少女已不见形影，原本立足之处只余下一枚锐利踏印。一闪而逝的剑光后，是突破音障的鸣啸……
t_en: In the next heartbeat, the lady disappeared. The spot she stood before was only left with a sharp footprint. With a flicker of a sword flash, comes a loud sonic boom...

t_ch: （以下章节需付费阅读）
t_en: (The following chapter requires payment to read)

t_ch: （放下小说）
t_en: (Put the novel down)

t_ch: 你们是谁，在这儿做什么？
t_en: Who are you and what are you doing here?

t_ch: 来得好…小崽子。就用你的血…浇灌仙迹。
t_en: You've come at the perfect time, little one. We can use your blood... to irrigate the celestial traces.

t_ch: …好吧，看来小爷非得给你个教训不可了。
t_en: "...Little one"? This little one is about to teach you a big lesson.

t_ch: 我会让你三招。
t_en: I'll let you make three moves.

t_ch: 出剑吧，让前辈久等，可是很失礼的。
t_en: Draw your sword. Don't you know it's rude to keep an elder waiting?

t_ch: 瞻前顾后，劲衰力弱。
t_en: Hesitant, weak.

t_ch: 你方才的自信到哪儿去了？
t_en: What happened to that confidence of yours?

t_ch: 这一击如何？
t_en: How was that?

t_ch: 不过如此。
t_en: Underwhelming.

t_ch: 到我了。
t_en: My turn.

t_ch: 要像这样，剑出无回，一击必杀。
t_en: Once you draw your sword you must strike down your adversary in one blow without turning back.

t_ch: 别想用言语乱我心境。
t_en: Keep quiet — I don't want your words distracting me.

t_ch: 景元教过你斩杀孽物…
t_en: Jing Yuan taught you how to slay abominations...

t_ch: …他有没有教你如何处置堕入魔阴身的仙舟人？
t_en: ...but did he ever teach you how to deal with the mara-struck?

t_ch: 答案是，并无区别。一剑贯穿丹腑，断其生息。
t_en: The answer is: There is no difference. The sword pierces the body and severs its life.

t_ch: 若这一剑向你刺来，你能否挡下？！
t_en: If this sword came for your throat, would you be able to deflect it!?

t_ch: 你以为剑术只是胜负的游戏么？未来的剑首？
t_en: Did you think this swordplay was just a game of victory and defeat, future Sword Champion?

t_ch: 够了！
t_en: Enough!

t_ch: 二人同行片刻，在一处驻足。
t_en: The two journey together before coming to a stop.

t_ch: 就从这儿开始好了。
t_en: May the best swordmaster win.

t_ch: 这里魔阴横行，妖氛遍布，正适合考校剑术。剑斩孽物，不违背云骑军的规纪，也谈不上「用公务与我交易」，对吧？
t_en: This place is brimming with mara — the perfect environment for honing one's skills. Surely slaying abominations doesn't count as "gambling with official business"?

t_ch: 怎么定胜负？
t_en: How do we tell who's won?

t_ch: 这一路到底，不可有漏网之鱼。先到者为胜。
t_en: We go from here to the end of the path without leaving a speck of abomination behind us. The first one to the end wins.

t_ch: 一言为定。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 你先行一步。
t_en: Lead the way.

t_ch: 对了，你该不会趁机转身逃跑吧，大姐姐？
t_en: What if you're just using this as a chance to escape?

t_ch: 景元真是把你给教坏了…尽耍嘴皮子。
t_en: Hmph, Jing Yuan trained you alright...

t_ch: 你是什么时候跑到前面去的？
t_en: I didn't even see you overtake me!

t_ch: 你好慢啊，小弟弟。
t_en: Too slow, young man.

t_ch: 人呢……？
t_en: Where did she go...?

t_ch: 这…这怎么可能？
t_en: But... how is that possible?

t_ch: 这一回是被什么事耽搁了？
t_en: What kept you this time?

t_ch: 可恶！
t_en: Argh!

t_ch: 太久没有动剑，一时兴起，险些勾起魔阴作孽。
t_en: A long time has passed since I wielded this sword... The heat of combat nearly cast me into the darkness.

t_ch: 来吧，小弟弟。余兴节目留给你了。
t_en: Come, young man — I'll let you finish up.

t_ch: 只有刚才那一剑，还不至于让人失望透顶。
t_en: Your final move... was the only one that didn't disappoint.

t_ch: …我输了。
t_en: ...I lost.

t_ch: 比试尚未结束，因为我的最后一剑还没来得及刺出，场上已没了对手……
t_en: Our contest has not yet come to a conclusion... for I have yet to make my final move, and the field has no opponent...

t_ch: 剑出鞘无功，亵渎帝弓司命的神意，至为不祥。
t_en: Unsheathing this sword without merit is to blaspheme the divine will of the Reignbow Arbiter, and invite calamity.
You...

t_ch: 以你的实力，就算遇见了刃，也不过死路一条。
t_en: Even with your strength, if you were to cross paths with Blade, it would mean death for you.

t_ch: 比起旦夕即死，我给你一个更体面的选择——在目睹我千锤百炼的一剑后，以剑士的身份赴死。
t_en: I can give you a more dignified end than dying at his hands... To die as a swordmaster having witnessed the perfection of my technique — refined a thousand times over.

t_ch: 小弟弟，你要不要，接我一剑？
t_en: What say you to that, young man?

t_ch: 不接！
t_en: I refuse!

t_ch: 不接！！
t_en: I refuse!!

t_ch: 不接！！！
t_en: I refuse!!!

t_ch: 好胆色。
t_en: You have courage.

t_ch: 「以此一剑，权作谢礼。因缘匪浅，他日重续。」
t_en: That move was a token of my appreciation. We were fated to meet this day, and in days to come.

t_ch: 这个来历不明的女人也想找到刃的下落……
t_en: This mysterious woman also wants to find the whereabouts of Blade...

t_ch: 不成，无论她想对犯人做什么，都绝不能任她捷足先登。
t_en: No, no matter what she wants to do with the fugitive, she must not be allowed to get to him first.

t_ch: 我必须加快脚步了……
t_en: I have to pick up the pace.

t_ch: 结束记录读取
t_en: Stop record checking

t_ch: 再等等
t_en: Hold on a moment

t_ch: 你可得好好努力了。
t_en: You'll need to work harder.

t_ch: 不然，给你一艘星槎也赶不上我。
t_en: Otherwise, you won't even catch me in a starskiff.

t_ch: 好久没有如此尽兴了……
t_en: I haven't had this much fun in a long time...

t_ch: 听说您找我有事？
t_en: I heard you require my assistance?

t_ch: 哦对了！瞧我这记性！您来了，{NICKNAME}！将军的贵客能把咱的求告听在耳中，真是不胜感谢！
t_en: Oh, that's right! Forgive my forgetfulness — glad you came, {NICKNAME}! It's great to have one of the general's distinguished guests helping us out.

t_ch: 之前您在长乐天广场中救下龙女，我代表照看不周的地衡司同袍，在此谢过您。
t_en: My Realm-Keeping Commission colleagues failed to provide adequate care for the Dragon Lady. I'd like to thank you on behalf of all of them for rescuing her back in Exalting Sanctum Plaza.

t_ch: 您客气了。
t_en: All in a day's work.

t_ch: 开门见山吧，有什么事？
t_en: Let's cut to the chase, shall we?

t_ch: 如您所见，自星核灾变降临，「魔阴身」的狂症就蔓延开来了。
t_en: As I'm sure you're aware, symptoms of mara have been spreading ever since the Stellaron incident occurred.

t_ch: 「魔阴身」是仙舟人一直以来所避忌的宿命，在十王司的守望下，它本不该如此频繁地出现。
t_en: Thanks to the Ten-Lords Commission, being stricken with mara had long since become a terrifying but avoidable fate for Xianzhou natives... The puzzling thing is that despite the Commission's protection, we're now seeing an increase.

t_ch: 可如今越来越多的人，尤其是云骑这样要承受压力与风险的人，开始堕入魔阴身。
t_en: More and more citizens — in particular those dealing with great risks and stress like the Cloud Knights — are becoming stricken with mara.

t_ch: 而且，那些人的样貌也变得极为可怖，形如怪物。这与我过往对「魔阴身」的认识不同……
t_en: Moreover, the mara-struck have taken on a horrifying appearance — as if they're turning into some sort of monster... This certainly differs from what I thought I knew about the illness.

t_ch: 所以，你要我消灭魔阴身？
t_en: So, you need me to eliminate the mara-struck?

t_ch: 我们岂敢拿这等事情劳烦你。抑制魔阴之事，将军自有计较。
t_en: We wouldn't have the audacity to trouble you with that — the general has a plan to suppress the illness.

t_ch: 所以，你怀疑背后有阴谋？
t_en: Do you think there might be a hidden agenda at play?

t_ch: 正是。所谓风起于青萍之末…我虽然只是地衡司一介小吏，但长期在一线工作，总能率先嗅到些危险的味道。
t_en: That appears to be the case — no smoke without fire, as they say... Despite being just a servant of the Realm-Keeping Commission, spending a considerable amount of time on the front lines has sharpened my senses to potential dangers...

t_ch: 您本事高强，受将军委托一路搜索星核，一定会与魔阴身交手……
t_en: You're a capable person, and since the general entrusted you with locating the Stellaron, it's only a matter of time before you run into mara-struck...

t_ch: 若是查到了什么蛛丝马迹，希望您能知会地衡司，一则我和同僚们能及时应对，二则，若有帮得上的忙，我也可以及早出力。
t_en: If you come across any clues, remember to inform the Realm-Keeping Commission straight away. My colleagues and I will handle any situation with haste and to the best of our ability.

t_ch: 对了，这一路上，您有什么发现吗？
t_en: Speaking of which, have you made any discoveries so far?

t_ch: 对付魔阴身时，我发现了这个…
t_en: When I was dealing with the mara-struck, I found this...

t_ch: 这是什么？这只是个普通的小物什。
t_en: Uh... What's this? Looks pretty ordinary to me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，请您别开玩笑了，在下最近正焦头烂额，忙的喘不过气来。
t_en: C'mon now, {NICKNAME}, this is a serious matter — there's no time for jokes.

t_ch: 大毫认真地阅读了你递来的信。接着，他露出难以置信的表情，又再读了一遍。
t_en: Dahao carefully reads the letter you delivered. His expression reflects his disbelief as he reads it again.

t_ch: 大毫满头大汗地向你告罪，匆匆离开。良久，他带回了一名女子。
t_en: Dahao sweats profusely as he apologizes and bolts away. After a while, he returns with a girl.

t_ch: 我…我看了您找到的那封信……
t_en: I... I read the letter...

t_ch: 坦白说，这个问题，我不知道该怎么处理。这事儿超出了地衡司所能管辖的范畴。所以，我联系了将军大人坐镇的神策府。
t_en: I'll be honest with you, I've got no idea how to handle this problem — it's beyond the Realm-Keeping Commission's jurisdiction. I contacted the Seat of Divine Foresight, which is overseen by the general.

t_ch: 我身边这位…这位是青镞大人，是景元将军的策士长。
t_en: ...Allow me to introduce Madam Qingzu, General Jing Yuan's chief counselor.

t_ch: 关于信的事情，相信由她来解释比较稳妥。
t_en: I think it'll be easier if she explains the letter to you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你从魔阴身处获得的信我已看过。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I read the letter you obtained from the mara-struck.

t_ch: 云骑军排查星核所在的同时，我也奉将军命令追索魔阴身相关之事。托你的福，线索已浮出水面了。
t_en: While the Cloud Knights were searching for the Stellaron's whereabouts, I was carrying out the general's orders — tracking down clues on the mara-struck. Thanks to your efforts, we now have a lead.

t_ch: 外人看来，这封信不过是某人久病得愈的家书喜讯。但里面所写的事情，实则触犯了「不赦十恶」的禁律，堪称大逆不道。
t_en: To an outsider, this letter appears to be nothing more than good news detailing a recovery from a chronic illness. However, what is actually being discussed is a violation of the Ten Unpardonable Sins. It's heresy.

t_ch: 你是指「慈怀药王」？
t_en: Are you talking about Merciful Medicus?

t_ch: 没错。慈怀药王，任何守法的联盟公民都不会念出这个敬称。
t_en: Correct. The term "Merciful Medicus" is one of respect — one that any law-abiding citizen of the Alliance would never dare utter.

t_ch: 因为它所称呼的，正是仙舟人的大敌，如今被我们称为「寿瘟祸祖」的丰饶星神，药师。
t_en: It refers to none other than the enemy of the people of the Xianzhou — the one we now call the Plagues Author... The Aeon of Abundance — Yaoshi.

t_ch: 你是指其中的药物实验？
t_en: Are you talking about the pharmacological experiment mentioned in the letter?

t_ch: 没错。在联盟成立后，仙舟上的一切医治行为都有严格的标准和界限，为的是避免借「治愈」之名求取长生或变异改造。
t_en: That's right. Since the Alliance was first established, all medical practices aboard the Xianzhou adhere to strict standards that prohibit long-life or mutant modifications under the guise of "curing."

t_ch: 信里提到的实验，我翻遍了卷宗，没有任何相关的记录。联想到丹鼎司的洞天被封锁了…其中显然有不可告人的秘密。
t_en: I went through all our files to find information on the experiment in question, and found nothing. I started thinking about how the Alchemy Commission's delve was sealed off... There must be an unspeakable secret locked away there.

t_ch: 这封信的作者，恐怕正是那个被你打败的魔阴身——他受人蛊惑，以治病为名遭毒害，成了你所见到的模样。
t_en: The author of this letter was likely the mara-struck you defeated. My guess is he was led astray and poisoned while under the pretext of being cured. You saw how he ended up...

t_ch: 似这等作派，令我想到了一个在仙舟上绝迹千年之久的组织…「药王秘传」。
t_en: Such actions remind me of an organization that was eradicated from the Xianzhou a thousand years ago... the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 以发掘禁忌丹方，复兴所谓的仙道之秘为志业的丰饶信徒。
t_en: They were disciples of the Abundance that sought to restore the way of immortality by unearthing a forbidden remedial formula.

t_ch: 没想到这样一个早已消亡的组织，如今竟然又死灰复燃，开始作乱人间了。神策府必须在事态变得不可挽回之前彻查这个组织当前的状况。
t_en: It's hard to believe a group that was wiped out so long ago would suddenly rear its head and create chaos once more. The Seat of Divine Foresight needs to learn all it can about this organization's status before the situation reaches a point of no return.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我有一事相求。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have a favor to ask of you.

t_ch: 如果接下来你还找到了类似的，可能与「药王秘传」有关的线索，请来神策府交给我。神策府现在很需要这样的第一手资料。
t_en: If you come across any similar clues about the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, come and find me at the Seat of Divine Foresight — these kinds of firsthand accounts are exactly what we require.

t_ch: 我能得到什么好处吗？
t_en: So, what do I get out of it?

t_ch: 放心好了，神策府不会亏待任何一个为我们做出过贡献的人。
t_en: Don't worry, the Seat of Divine Foresight always rewards those who contribute to our efforts.

t_ch: 好啊，在所不辞。
t_en: Sounds good to me.

t_ch: 那太好了，你绝不会做白工的。神策府不会亏待任何一个为我们做出过贡献的人。
t_en: Excellent. Your work will not be in vain. The Seat of Divine Foresight always rewards those who contribute to our efforts.

t_ch: 听起来很麻烦…
t_en: Sounds like one big headache to me...

t_ch: 我理解。但只要你有空时留意一下就好。神策府不会亏待任何一个为我们做出过贡献的人。
t_en: I understand, but all I'm asking is for you to keep an eye out when you have time. The Seat of Divine Foresight always rewards those who contribute to our efforts.

t_ch: 我最近接获线报，趁着眼下局势混乱，长乐天一带似乎有人在蛊惑化外民。也许你可以从那里开始？
t_en: I recently received some intel. It seems that certain individuals are taking advantage of the current chaos to lure in outworlders around Exalting Sanctum. Maybe you could start there?

t_ch: 实际上，在几个月前我曾派出了一位调查人员加以刺探，有了些眉目。
t_en: We sent an undercover investigator out a few months ago — they provided us with a rough idea of the situation.

t_ch: 但对方似乎也起了疑心…卧底失联了。我疑心他是被人认了出来……
t_en: Unfortunately, it seems suspicions were aroused... We lost contact with the agent — I suspect he was discovered...

t_ch: 你是罗浮上的生面孔，还是化外民，这件事交给你来办，我放心很多。
t_en: You are a fresh face on the Luofu and an outworlder. I would feel more at ease if I could leave this matter with you.

t_ch: 与这些妖人接触，难免会有危险，若你实在不想去，我也不会强求。但要是能带回些有价值的情报，神策府必定重重酬谢。
t_en: Danger is practically an inevitability when dealing with these devils. If you really don't want to go, I won't force you... but I repeat — if you are able to return with valuable intelligence, the Seat of Divine Foresight will reward you handsomely.

t_ch: 这位异邦的客人，请留步，你渴望长生吗？
t_en: Excuse me, guest from another world. Please spare me a moment of your time. Do you yearn for immortality?

t_ch: 不渴望。
t_en: No, I don't.

t_ch: 不渴望？也许是因为你对此已然绝望！
t_en: Do you not believe in the pursuit of eternal life? Perhaps it's because you've already given up on that aspiration!

t_ch: 谁会拒绝永远活下去的机会呢？只是仙舟全然不在乎短生种的福祉啊！他们固执地禁绝走向长生的光荣之路。
t_en: Who would turn down the opportunity to live forever? The Xianzhou doesn't care one bit about the well-being of short-life species! They stubbornly blockade the glorious path to immortality.

t_ch: 渴望。
t_en: Yes, I do.

t_ch: 说的好！谁会拒绝永远活下去的机会呢？
t_en: Excellent! Who would turn down the opportunity to live forever?

t_ch: 只是仙舟全然不在乎短生种的福祉啊！他们固执地禁绝走向长生的光荣之路。
t_en: The Xianzhou doesn't care one bit about the well-being of short-life species! They stubbornly blockade the glorious path to immortality.

t_ch: 我渴望力量！
t_en: I yearn for power!

t_ch: 我明白了！你是说你渴望长生，对吧！毕竟长生不死也是一种力量！
t_en: I understand! Eternal life is indeed a kind of power, wouldn't you say?

t_ch: 不，我只是渴望纯粹的力量！
t_en: No. I want power, pure and simple.
Uh...

t_ch: 你把我给整不会了，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}。
t_en: I'm at my wit's end with you, {F#Sister}{M#Brother}.

t_ch: 哈哈，你这个人真有意思，坦率讲吧，我这儿只能为化外民提供长生不死的机会，但也许你更适合加入反物质军团。
t_en: Haha, what a peculiar person you are. I'll tell it to you straight — to outworlders I offer the chance of immortality... but perhaps someone like you would be better off joining the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 无论选哪个，都让咱们把声线放低一些。这都不是体面的话题。
t_en: Regardless of what you choose, let's keep our voices down... eh? This isn't the kind of conversation you can have out in the open.

t_ch: 在获得绝对的力量前，你一定也是渴望永远活下去的吧？但仙舟联盟不在乎短生种的福祉！他们固执地禁绝走向长生的光荣之路。
t_en: Before you can grasp absolute power, you must yearn for immortality, don't you think? The problem is that the Xianzhou Alliance doesn't care one bit about the well-being of short-life species! They stubbornly blockade the glorious path to immortality.

t_ch: 我叫绿芙蓉。此番来到长乐天，就是希望能邂逅到像你这样的有缘人，和我同登长生仙道。
t_en: My name is Green Hibiscus. I came to Exalting Sanctum in hopes of a fated encounter with one such as yourself — one who is also on the road to immortality.

t_ch: 怎么样？跟我一起去个更适合讨论这些事的地方吧？
t_en: What do you say? How about we find a more suitable spot to talk about all this?

t_ch: 行啊，走呗。
t_en: Sure, let's go.

t_ch: 咱们出发吧，这里人太多，不宜久留。
t_en: Let's make haste — there are too many people here to linger.

t_ch: 听起来很危险…
t_en: Sounds pretty dangerous...

t_ch: 哎！怎么会呢！我们都是些老实本分的好市民啊，只是比其他仙舟住民更忠实于内心的渴望罢了。我不会害你的。
t_en: Bah, how so? We are dutiful and sincere — the only difference between us and other Xianzhou residents is that we are more faithful to our inner desires. I won't harm you.

t_ch: 怎么，你想明白了？
t_en: So, made up your mind?

t_ch: 走吧，你最好别骗我。
t_en: Yes, but don't even think about lying to me.

t_ch: 咱们出发吧，这里人太多，不宜久留。
t_en: Good, then let's make haste — there are too many people here to linger.

t_ch: 不了，我不习惯跟陌生人走。
t_en: It's a no — I don't make a habit of following strangers around.

t_ch: 绿芙蓉轻松地吹了个唿哨，用笃定的眼神看向你，他似乎认定了你一定会屈从内心的渴望跟他走。
t_en: Green Hibiscus gently whistles. He looks at you with a calm expression, seemingly certain that you will give into your heart's desire and go with him.

t_ch: 到地方了，就是这里。
t_en: Here we are. This is the place.

t_ch: 怎么获得长生？
t_en: How do I gain immortality?

t_ch: 哎，先别急嘛。要是人人都能轻而易举获得长生，我们早就发展壮大，也不必那么掩人耳目了。
t_en: Ah, let's not get ahead of ourselves. If it were so easy, we'd be a far larger group, and there'd be no need for secrecy.

t_ch: 你们要干什么？
t_en: What are you up to?

t_ch: 你放心好了，药王慈怀，我是不会害你的。
t_en: Relax. By the mercy of Sanctus Medicus, I will not harm you.

t_ch: 重新自我介绍一下，我是药王秘传的「莳者」。之前在长乐天不便暴露身份，但现在可以放心大胆地告诉你们了。
t_en: Allow me to reintroduce myself... I am a member of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus. I was unable to expose my true identity in Exalting Sanctum, but now I can reveal everything.

t_ch: 「药王秘传」，是奉慈怀药王为正教正法的组织，而「莳者」，就是我们这些追随者们的自称。
t_en: The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus is a devout group, holding firm in their belief that Merciful Medicus is the one, ultimate faith. As followers, we refer to ourselves as disciples, dedicated to our cause.

t_ch: 想获取长生之法，就要成为一名莳者，但想成为莳者，还要经历一些小小考验。
t_en: If you wish to gain the means to immortality, then you must become a disciple... but to become a disciple, you must first pass several small trials.

t_ch: 请各位见谅…随着药王秘传逐渐壮大，也引起了那些妖弓信徒的注意，若不这样小心行事，后果不堪设想啊。
t_en: Please excuse me... Our expansion has unfortunately attracted the attention of those Devilish Archer followers. If we are not careful, the consequences could be unthinkable.

t_ch: 这个云骑为什么在这里…
t_en: What is this Cloud Knight doing here...?

t_ch: 不用担心这个云骑。他和你一样，在接受考验。
t_en: No need to be concerned about him. Much like yourself, he is here to undergo the trials.

t_ch: 仙舟人不是恨「丰饶」吗？
t_en: Don't the people of the Xianzhou abhor Abundance?

t_ch: 哈哈，只有那些被妖弓蛊惑之人，才会仇视慈怀药王。但很遗憾，这样的人很多…实在是太多了。
t_en: Haha. Only those led astray by followers of the Devilish Archer could despise Merciful Medicus. Sadly, there are many such people... too many.

t_ch: 我能从药王那儿得到什么？
t_en: What can I get from Sanctus Medicus?

t_ch: 每个种族都会渴求慈怀药王的恩惠与治愈，无论他们是否听说过「丰饶」的名字。
t_en: Every species yearns for the favor and curing of Merciful Medicus, regardless of whether they have heard of the Abundance or not.

t_ch: 狐人渴求「治愈」仅有三百年的寿元。持明渴求「治愈」轮回和绝嗣之苦……
t_en: Foxians yearn to cure themselves of their <unbreak>300</unbreak>-year lifespan. The Vidyadhara yearn to cure themselves of the pain of reincarnation and lack of progeny...

t_ch: 而仙舟人，他们渴求「治愈」魔阴身的折磨——虽然我们认为那并非折磨，更像是某种进化的恩赐。
t_en: ...As for Xianzhou natives — they long to cure themselves of the torture of becoming mara-struck. Of course, we know that this "torture" is rather a kind of blessed evolution.

t_ch: 回到你的问题，药王能「治愈」你们短生种的衰老、病痛和死亡，就像祂对仙舟人所做的那样。
t_en: To return to your question, Sanctus Medicus can cure the aging that short-life species face, along with disease and death. Sanctus Medicus can do for you what THEY did for the Xianzhou natives.

t_ch: 我要接受什么样的考验？
t_en: What kind of trial do I have to pass?

t_ch: 「令诸所求，必有所得」。慈怀药王心胸宽广，绝不会轻易拒绝任何人的祈愿。所以我们药王秘传的入门考验也不会太刁难人。
t_en: "What you seek, you will surely find." Merciful Medicus is magnanimous — they do not lightly refuse a prayer. As such, the initiation trials for the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus are nothing arduous.

t_ch: 只要将这册《千手慈怀药王救世品》抄写五百遍，让兄弟姐妹们相信你的诚意，就可以让你成为一名莳者了。
t_en: All you must do is transcribe this copy of "Thousand-Handed Merciful Medicus' Salvation" <unbreak>500</unbreak> times by hand. This will allow your brothers and sisters to trust your faith... and you to become a disciple.

t_ch: 这可是咱们药王秘传的根本大经，你要怀着虔敬的心情认真抄写…最好还能背下来。
t_en: This scripture is central to the Disciples — you must maintain a pious heart during the transcription... It would be best if you could memorize the text.

t_ch: 当然了，这只是第一步。成为莳者之后，你还要经过很多其他的考验。
t_en: Of course, this is but the first trial. Once you have become a disciple, you will undergo numerous others.

t_ch: 五百遍也太多了…
t_en: <unbreak>500</unbreak> times seems excessive...

t_ch: 多吗？以前的规矩可是五万遍，我当年就抄了五万遍。现在是魁首大人疼惜苍生疾苦，才特地改成了五百遍。
t_en: Excessive? The old rules dictated that the text be copied <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> times — I myself undertook such a feat. Nowadays, the master takes greater pity on the common people, hence the number has been reduced to a mere <unbreak>500</unbreak>.

t_ch: 你给我笔了吗？
t_en: Can I get a pen?

t_ch: 放心吧，都给你准备好了。传统的仙舟笔墨纸砚，书写流畅，符合人体工学，抄写五万遍都不会觉得累。是真的，我当初就抄了五万遍。
t_en: Don't worry, a traditional Xianzhou brush and inkstone has been prepared for you — smooth action and an ergonomic design. I myself transcribed <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> copies with such instruments.

t_ch: 你要去哪儿？试炼还没结束，你可不能走啊。
t_en: Where are you going? The trial isn't over, you can't just leave!

t_ch: 嗯？怎么才一走神，你就跑出去了？快回来，试炼还没结束，你可不能走啊。
t_en: Hm? Your mind might've wandered, but that doesn't mean you can. Come back and finish the trial!

t_ch: 你总算过来了…我认得你，你是{NICKNAME}！景元将军的贵客！
t_en: Took you long enough... I know you — you're {NICKNAME}, General Jing Yuan's honored guest!

t_ch: 你认错人了…
t_en: I think you've got the wrong person...

t_ch: 你相信我好不好！我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: You're here undercover, right? So am I! You've got to believe me!

t_ch: 井圆是谁…
t_en: Jim Yan? Never heard of him...

t_ch: 哎呀，都这个时候了，就别说冷笑话了…我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: *sigh* Is this really the time for jokes of that quality... You're here undercover, right? Well, so am I!

t_ch: 看来只能灭口了…
t_en: Looks like I'll have to make sure no one finds out...

t_ch: 别别别…我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: W—Wait a second! You're here undercover, right? So am I!

t_ch: 我是长乐天卫戍区的云骑，见到那个「绿芙蓉」一直鬼鬼祟祟的，还宣传一些离经叛道的思想，这才跟过来，准备卧底。
t_en: I'm a Cloud Knight from the Exalting Sanctum garrison. That Green Hibiscus has been sneaking around spreading heretical lies for a while now. That's why I'm here — to spy on him!

t_ch: 没想到啊，一来就被他们怀疑了…费了好大的力气才勉强让他们相信我是真心想加入药王秘传的……
t_en: They were suspicious of me as soon as I arrived... It took a lot of effort to make them believe I wanted to become a disciple...

t_ch: 你怎么穿着军装就来了…
t_en: Why did you come here in your uniform...?

t_ch: 你穿成这样那必然会被怀疑啊…
t_en: Of course, they're bound to have their doubts when you're dressed like that...

t_ch: 嗐，我寻摸着像这样的邪恶组织发展下线时，多半希望成员能在执法者队伍里渗透影响力，所以我穿着盔甲就来了。这叫充分洞察用户需求。
t_en: Hmph, I have my reasoning. Cults like this want to recruit people with influence in law and order — I wanted them to know I was a Cloud Knight! Always know your enemy...

t_ch: 你还真是个天才…
t_en: You really are a genius...

t_ch: 那你现在想怎样？
t_en: What's your next plan?

t_ch: 嘘，说话小声些…这帮家伙可不是吃素的，要是让他们发觉咱俩是卧底，那一个也跑不了。
t_en: Shh, keep your voice down — these disciples don't miss much. If they discover us, we won't make it out of here.

t_ch: 先说好了，我帮你抄完经，你可要帮我打掩护，我得去神策府通风报信……
t_en: Listen carefully — I help you copy the text, you cover for me, and I get word out to the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 来，我来替你抄经。当云骑之前，我在神策府干了一百五十多年的文职工作，抄写对我而言简直是小菜一碟。
t_en: You can trust me with the transcription. I spent over <unbreak>150</unbreak> years pen-pushing in the Seat of Divine Foresight before I became a Cloud Knight — I could do this in my sleep.

t_ch: 趁着那些看守你们的莳者不注意，这位云骑奋笔疾书，很快就将经文抄好了。
t_en: The Cloud Knight swiftly scribbles away and finishes copying the sacred text while the disciples watching over you aren't looking.

t_ch: 你开始抄写这册《千手慈怀药王救世品》。
t_en: You start copying the Thousand-Handed Merciful Medicus' Salvation.

t_ch: 「一时千手慈怀药王游化诸界，与莳者九万九千九百九十九人俱，住罗睺下，而为说法。」
t_en: "Once upon a time, the thousand-hand Sanctus Medicus traveled the world with their <unbreak>99,999</unbreak> disciples, living under Rahu and teaching their Way."

t_ch: 你的食指有些疼，肩膀也有些酸…可这才是第一遍。还有四百九十九遍在等待着你。
t_en: Your index finger has a slight cramp, and your shoulders feel a bit sore... But you've only made the first copy. There are still <unbreak>499</unbreak> copies waiting to be written.

t_ch: 「说是语时，莳者九万九千九百九十九人俱，皆断生老病苦。」
t_en: "As they spoke the words, the <unbreak>99,999</unbreak> disciples were liberated from youth, old age, sickness, and suffering."

t_ch: 在落下最后一笔时，你眼角的余光瞥到那个云骑正在向你打手势。
t_en: After making the final stroke, you catch the Cloud Knight in your peripheral vision as he gestures at you.

t_ch: 过来，过来，别抄了，快过来。
t_en: Psst, hey, stop writing — get over here!

t_ch: 你低下头，对周围的一切充耳不闻，静静地抄写着。
t_en: You lower your head. You become closed off to the world around you as you quietly copy the text.

t_ch: 你的手臂越来越疼，但终究还是抄完了第二遍。
t_en: Your hand and shoulder are experiencing more pain, but you've only produced two copies.

t_ch: 「药王慈怀，建木生发。莳者一心，同登极乐。」
t_en: "Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, the Arbor's new growth. Unity of Discipuli Medici, the Enlightenment's true path."

t_ch: 抄写第三遍时，这句话开始在你的心中回响。
t_en: You finish the third copy. This sentence has started to reverberate within your being.

t_ch: 你的手臂和手指已经不疼了。可能是习惯了，也可能是麻木了。
t_en: Your hand and shoulder no longer hurt. Maybe you've gotten used to the work, or perhaps they have grown numb.

t_ch: 你顺利地抄完了第四遍。
t_en: You complete the fourth copy.

t_ch: 在抄写第五遍时，旁边的那位云骑终于按捺不住，悄悄蹭到了你的身边。
t_en: As you write out the fifth copy, the Cloud Knight near you no longer can wait in silence. He quietly comes up next to you.

t_ch: 别抄了！别抄了！你还抄上瘾了是吧！我来替你抄完吧，你来帮我望风！
t_en: Stop writing — you wanna fall under their spell? I'll finish it for you — go keep a lookout!

t_ch: 趁着那些看守你们的莳者不注意，这位云骑奋笔疾书，很快就将经文抄好了。
t_en: The Cloud Knight swiftly scribbles away and finishes copying the sacred text while the disciples watching over you aren't looking.

t_ch: 行了行了，终于抄完了…我认得你，你是{NICKNAME}！景元将军的贵客！
t_en: Alright, finally... I know you — you're {NICKNAME}, General Jing Yuan's honored guest!

t_ch: 你认错人了…
t_en: I think you've got the wrong person...

t_ch: 你相信我好不好！我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: You're here undercover, right? So am I! You've got to believe me!

t_ch: 井圆是谁…
t_en: Jim Yan? Never heard of him...

t_ch: 哎呀，都这个时候了，就别说冷笑话了…我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: *sigh* Is this really the time for jokes of that quality... You're here undercover, right? Well, so am I!

t_ch: 看来只能灭口了…
t_en: Looks like I'll have to make sure no one finds out...

t_ch: 别别别…我知道，你肯定是来卧底的。我其实也是！
t_en: W—Wait a second! You're here undercover, right? So am I!

t_ch: 我是长乐天卫戍区的云骑，见到那个「绿芙蓉」一直鬼鬼祟祟的，还宣传一些离经叛道的思想，这才跟过来，准备卧底。
t_en: I'm a Cloud Knight from the Exalting Sanctum garrison. That Green Hibiscus has been sneaking around spreading heretical lies for a while now. That's why I'm here — to spy on him!

t_ch: 没想到啊，一来就被他们怀疑了…费了好大的力气才勉强让他们相信我是真心想加入药王秘传的……
t_en: They were suspicious of me as soon as I arrived... It took a lot of effort to make them believe I wanted to become a disciple...

t_ch: 你怎么穿着军装就来了…
t_en: Why did you come here in your uniform...?

t_ch: 你穿成这样那必然会被怀疑啊…
t_en: Of course, they're bound to have their doubts when you're dressed like that...

t_ch: 嗐，我寻摸着像这样的邪恶组织发展下线时，多半希望成员能在执法者队伍里渗透影响力，所以我穿着盔甲就来了。这叫充分洞察用户需求。
t_en: Hmph, I have my reasoning. Cults like this want to recruit people with influence in law and order — I wanted them to know I was a Cloud Knight! Always know your enemy...

t_ch: 你还真是个天才…
t_en: You really are a genius...

t_ch: 那你现在想怎样？
t_en: What's your next plan?

t_ch: 嘘，说话小声些…这帮家伙可不是吃素的，要是让他们发觉咱俩是卧底，那一个也跑不了。
t_en: Shh, keep your voice down — these disciples don't miss much. If they discover us, we won't make it out of here.

t_ch: 先说好了，我帮你抄完经，你可要帮我打掩护，我得去神策府通风报信……
t_en: Listen carefully — I help you copy the text, you cover for me, and I get word out to the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 请静下心来，慢慢抄写，慢慢体悟《千手慈怀药王救世品》至善至美的启迪。
t_en: Take your time, still your mind, and transcribe the text. Allow the beautiful enlightenment of Thousand-Handed Merciful Medicus' Salvation to permeate your being.

t_ch: 我抄完了。
t_en: I've finished it.

t_ch: 好的，再等一下。
t_en: Okay. Just a moment.

t_ch: 已经抄完了？这么快？我检查一下…嗯…嗯…嗯…还真是都抄完了，还抄得又快又好。看来你是颇有仙缘的。
t_en: You're finished? So soon? Let me see... Hmm... Hmm... Yes, it would appear you are finished — a fast and accurate transcription. It seems you are destined for immortality.

t_ch: 我现在能加入药王秘传了？
t_en: So, can I join the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus now?

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，稍安勿躁…入门试炼分为两步，你和那位云骑已经完成了第一步。现在…还剩下最后一步。
t_en: Calm yourself... The initiation is divided into two steps — you and the Cloud Knight have completed the first. Now... only the second remains.

t_ch: 好啊，那这下一步等我们明天再来吧？我现在是勤务时间，偷偷跑出来的，再不回去他们会起疑心的。
t_en: Sure... but can it wait till tomorrow? I'm supposed to be on duty right now — they'll get suspicious if I don't head back soon.

t_ch: 不必担心。第二步非常简单啊，很快就可以结束了。
t_en: No need to worry, the second step is extraordinarily simple. It'll all be over soon enough.

t_ch: 咱们药王秘传的用人原则是，疑人不用、用人不疑。所以……
t_en: The Disciples' initiation is governed by the following principle — never accept who you suspect, and never suspect who you accept. So...

t_ch: 请二位在此处决斗，活下来的人，就可以正式成为一名莳者。
t_en: ...it's time for you two to battle it out. Whoever survives will officially become a disciple.

t_ch: 为什么啊？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 抄经可以判断出二位的虔敬之心，但是否对组织忠诚，还需要一些更有力的保障啊…没办法，规矩也不是我定的。
t_en: Transcribing scripture allows us to ascertain your devotion, but determining whether or not you'll be loyal to the organization requires a stronger guarantee... Sorry, there's nothing I can do about it, I didn't write the rules.

t_ch: 我才不要…
t_en: I'll pass...

t_ch: 没办法，规矩也不是我定的。如果不肯完成试炼，那我们也只能委屈二位永远保守秘密了……
t_en: There is nothing that can be done. I didn't write the rules. If you are unwilling to complete the initiation process, then we will have to ask that you keep our secrets hidden... permanently...

t_ch: 好，战吧！
t_en: Okay. Let's fight!

t_ch: 好啊！药王秘传就喜欢你这种有干劲的新鲜血液！来吧，让我们看看你的本事吧！
t_en: Excellent! The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus loves enthusiastic new blood! Well then, show us what you're made of!

t_ch: 等一下，先等一下…别啊，我们是为求长生才来的，又不是为寻死才来的，这样突然让我们决斗，我们很难办啊！
t_en: Wh—Whoa, whoa — you're kidding, right? We came here to lengthen our lifespans, not shorten them! You can't just make us fight like this!

t_ch: 倒也不是强制你们决斗，不肯斗也可以，但那样就不可能放你们任何人离开了。
t_en: We aren't forcing you to fight — by all means refuse if you wish... but we won't possibly be able to let either of you leave.

t_ch: 好的…好的…那让我先做一下心理准备，这总可以吧？我以前也没干过这种事啊……
t_en: I... Fine, fine — but at least let me prepare. I've never done anything like this before...

t_ch: 我倒是无所谓，这要问你的对手……
t_en: Very well, but you'll need to ask your opponent, too...

t_ch: 我也无所谓。
t_en: I don't mind either.

t_ch: 好的，那你们准备一下吧。时间宝贵，别准备太久。
t_en: Good. Then prepare yourselves. Time is precious — make it quick.
